primary_change.factual_summary: FIRST POST-EOM-CLOSURE POLL. All 8 April-EOM PRED windows closed at 2026-04-30T23:59Z (~11.13h before poll). NET QUIET on dome public surface — all 6 covered page hashes BYTE-IDENTICAL to prior poll baseline (home, wins, predictions, coordinates, model, evolution). 23rd consecutive byte-identical predictions.html / ~10.86 days. 9 commits in 12.01h, ALL auto-only — 6x Auto-update ECM status + 2x auto: ECM data pull + 1x auto: daily AI review (152383a 07:37:58Z = HOLD #10 regen). HOLD #10 modified docs/data/daily_review.json (+5/-5) and docs/tracking.html (+3/-11) ONLY; NO public page touched; registry counts UNCHANGED (confirmed=69, prospective_confirmed=10, refined=4, accuracy=94.5%, V51.1, last_updated=2026-04-17). Manual-commit drought 13.96d / 335.17h / 25 consecutive auto-only polls. 0 source-file SHA changes (monitor.py, scripts/pull_data.py, scripts/build_daily_review.py, inject_ai_layer.py, verify_predictions.py, generate_manifest.js all identical). Canary OTS-provenance trap UNCHANGED (same 4 pre-existing .ots files, no new prediction-isolation OTS, 0 canary-keyword hits in last 9 commits). Content-security sweep clean (0 prompt-injection phrases, 0 hidden-CSS anomalies). Status-badge enumeration on predictions.html unchanged: only 3 CONFIRMED rows total, no new CONFIRMED/REFINED/FALSIFIED transitions on any of the 8 EOM PREDs or PRED-105.

kernel_of_truth.description: The load-bearing diagnostic flagged at the prior analyst run (2026-05-01T00:03Z) was: would HOLD #10 — the first daily-AI-review regen scheduled to fire after the 8 EOM closures — adjudicate any of those closures, or would it continue the do-not-adjudicate pattern? Answer: HOLD #10 fired at 07:37:58Z (~7.6h post-closure), modified daily_review.json with +5/-5 (so the pipeline IS regenerating commentary, not broken-cron stale), and produced ZERO change to any registry count. Combined with 11h of silent-ignore on all 8 EOM PREDs and 25 consecutive auto-only polls, this is direct evidence that the do-not-adjudicate pattern is now baked into the dome’s automated commentary pipeline, not just author-side inaction. The first post-closure regen was the cleanest test we could have asked for, and it returned the (a)-favoring result.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_matters: The (a) vs (b) split from the prior analysis — (a) sustained disengagement / do-not-adjudicate as stable behavior, (b) batched-edit pre-staging — now has its first piece of substantive evidence pointing at (a). The next 24-72h is still load-bearing (pattern (b) batched-edit could yet land), but pattern (a) has gained probability mass. Concretely: the dome’s daily-AI-review pipeline ran on first opportunity post-closure and refused to update registry counts. That is a behavioral signal about the *infrastructure*, not the author’s manual choices, which makes it harder to explain away as authorial procrastination.

current_review_text_affected.summary: NONE. No WIN-level claim is touched. No section prose requires update. The PRED-105 silent_ignore writeup remains current. The canary-page narrative remains current.

strategic_assessment.is_responding_to_our_review: No movement to evaluate. Net-quiet poll; no public-surface bytes changed; HOLD #10 produced no registry change. Author-contact prior cannot be applied to a non-event.
strategic_assessment.falsifiability_implications: HOLD #10 firing without adjudication on first post-closure regen reduces the model’s falsifiability claim — if the dome’s own automated review pipeline does not adjudicate predictions when their windows close, then the displayed registry counts (69 confirmed / 4 refined / 94.5%) cannot be receiving update pressure from prediction outcomes. That is, the registry is not a function of the prediction record; it is decoupled from it. We have already documented this de-coupling in our review (Step 10b silent-ignore framing); this poll provides additional confirmation.
strategic_assessment.other_side_of_argument: Counter-arguments to the HOLD #10 = stable do-not-adjudicate reading: (1) HOLD #10 could be a degraded-but-still-running cron rather than a deliberate choice. Counter: daily_review.json had +5/-5 modification, so content IS regenerating; the registry-update step is what is selectively not happening. (2) 11h post-closure is too short to declare silent-ignore confirmed. Counter: yes — that is precisely why the poller held this at MEDIUM not HIGH, why pattern (a)/(b) remains 24-72h-load-bearing, and why we are not advancing any verdicts today. (3) Could a single batched manual commit still land in the next 24-72h flipping all 8 closures + PRED-105 at once? Counter: zero pre-staging activity in 25 polls, no draft commits, no force-push, no non-main-branch activity visible via the GitHub API. The author-contact prior raises the prior on responsive moves but does not specifically forecast batched closure adjudication. (4) Could the registry counts be sourced from somewhere outside our monitoring? Counter: ai_manifest.json byte-identical 405d5c93…, api/index.json byte-stable e00e85aa…, no source-file SHA changes — the registry-display surface is fully covered.

next_run_guidance_for_analyst: Next poll likely lands ~2026-05-01T23:07Z. (2) PRED-105 d crosses 14.0+ — analyst should advance silent_ignore → tacit_withdrawal at next-day poll (~12.0Z 2026-05-02 or first poll thereafter). (3) Any manual commit (would break the 14d+ drought immediately upon next-day poll) — classify per author-contact prior, content-match dome-specific topics. (4) Any new OTS file referencing predictions-only (not status_history.json) → CANARY TRIGGERED, escalate immediately. (5) HOLD #11 daily-AI-review regen due ~07:35Z 2026-05-02; if it again produces no adjudication change, the do-not-adjudicate pattern strengthens to 11 consecutive regens spanning the EOM closure boundary. (6) Sq-subtraction methodology document per analyst’s 04-27 EXP-267 guidance — if it appears OTS-anchored before measurement, that IS the canary fix. (7) Source-file SHA change → CRITICAL.
